name: pycondor
documentation:
# PyCondor

[![Build Status](https://github.com/pycondor/pycondor/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/pycondor/pycondor/actions/workflows/test.yml)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/pycondor/pycondor/branch/master/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/pycondor/pycondor)
![pypi version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pycondor.svg 'pypi version')
![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/pycondor.svg)
![license](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/pycondor.svg 'license')

PyCondor (Python HTCondor) is a tool to help build and submit workflows to HTCondor in a straight-forward manner with minimal hassle.


## Documentation

The documentation for PyCondor can be found at https://pycondor.github.io/pycondor.


## Installation


PyCondor can be installed using `pip`

```
pip install pycondor
```

or `conda`

```
conda install -c conda-forge pycondor
```

For more information see the [installation instructions](https://pycondor.github.io/pycondor/installation.html) in the [documentation](https://pycondor.github.io/pycondor).


## Contributing

To contribute to PyCondor, please see the [contributing guide](https://pycondor.github.io/pycondor/contributing.html) in the documentation.


## License

[MIT License](LICENSE)

Copyright (c) 2018 James Bourbeau

functions.name: pycondor.utils._setup_logger
documentation:
Configures and returns logger instance.

This function takes a class instance (which must have a `name` attribute)
and a verbosity level as parameters and returns a connfigured logger
instance with the appropriate level set.

Parameters
----------
cls : object
    Class instance with attribute `name` (e.g. Dagman, Job)
verbose: int
    Verbosity level. Values can be 0, 1, or 2, with 0 being the least
    verbose and 2 being the most verbose.

Returns
-------
logger : logging.Logger
    Configured logger

Allows classes to each have their own seperate loggers in order to
allow for varying levels of verbosity. For example, you might want low
verbosity for a pycondor Job, but high verbosity for a Dagman
class. setup_logger() helps streamline this process.

functions.name: pycondor.dagman._iter_job_args
documentation:
Iterates over Job args list. Yields the name (and JobArg) for each node
to be used when adding job to a Dagman (i.e. the name in the
'JOB name job_submit_file' line).

# Parameters

job

: Job to iterate over. Note that the submit file for job must be built
  prior to using \_iter_job_args.

# Yields

node_name

: Node name to use in Dagman object.

job_arg

: Job argument object (`arg`, `name`, `retry` attributes).

methods.name: pycondor.dagman.Dagman.build
documentation:
Build and saves the submit file for Dagman

# Parameters

makedirs

: If Job directories (e.g. error, output, log, submit) don't exist,
  create them (default is `True`).

fancyname

: Appends the date and unique id number to error, log, output, and
  submit files. For example, instead of `dagname.submit` the submit
  file becomes `dagname_YYYYMMD_id`. This is useful when running
  several Dags/Jobs of the same name (default is `True`).

# Returns

self

: Returns self.

  (see the [condor_submit_dag documentation](http://research.cs.wisc.edu/htcondor/manual/current/condor_submit_dag.html)
  for possible options).

# Returns

self

: Returns self.

documentation:

# Parameters

makedirs

: If Job directories (e.g. error, output, log, submit) don't exist,
  create them (default is `True`).

fancyname

: Appends the date and unique id number to error, log, output, and
  submit files. For example, instead of `dagname.submit` the submit
  file becomes `dagname_YYYYMMD_id`. This is useful when running
  several Dags/Jobs of the same name (default is `True`).

submit_options

: Options to be passed to `condor_submit_dag` for this Dagman
  (see the [condor_submit_dag documentation](http://research.cs.wisc.edu/htcondor/manual/current/condor_submit_dag.html)
  for possible options).

# Returns

self

: Returns self.

methods.name: pycondor.dagman.Dagman.visualize
documentation:
Visualize Dagman graph

# Parameters

filename

: File to save graph diagram to. If `None` then no file is saved.
  Valid file extensions are 'png', 'pdf', 'dot', 'svg', 'jpeg', 'jpg'.

Dagman object consisting of a series of Jobs and sub-Dagmans to manage.

Note that the `submit` parameter can be explicitly given or configured
by setting the `PYCONDOR_SUBMIT_DIR` environment variable. An explicitly
given value for `submit` will be used over the environment variable,
while the environment variable will be used over a default value.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the Dagman instance. This will also be the name of the
  corresponding error, log, output, and submit files associated with
  this Dagman.

submit

: Path to directory where condor dagman submit files will be written
  (defaults to the directory was the Dagman was submitted from).

extra_lines

: List of additional lines to be added to submit file.

  :::{versionadded} 0.1.1
  :::

dag

: If specified, Dagman will be added to dag as a subdag
  (default is None).

verbose

: Level of logging verbosity option are 0-warning, 1-info,
  2-debugging (default is 0).

# Attributes

jobs

: The list of jobs for this Dagman instance to manage.

parents

: List of parent Jobs and Dagmans. Ensures that Jobs and Dagmans in the
  parents list will complete before this Dagman is submitted to HTCondor.

children

: List of child Jobs and Dagmans. Ensures that Jobs and Dagmans in the
  children list will be submitted only after this Dagman has completed.

functions.name: pycondor.cli.progress_bar_str
documentation:
Function to convert a Status object into a progress bar string

Parameters
----------
status : Status
    Status namedtuple that contains the current number of jobs that are
    done, queued, ready, failed, etc.
datetime_start : datetime.datetime
    Datetime for the first line of the dagman out file.
datetime_current : datetime.datetime
    Datetime for the current line of the dagman out file.
length : int, optional
    Width of the progress bar (default is 30).
prog_char : str, optional
    Character used to fill the progress bar (default is '#').

Returns
-------
prog_bar_str : str
    Progress bar string.

classes.name: pycondor.job.Job

methods.name: pycondor.job.Job.add_arg
documentation:
Add argument to Job

# Parameters

arg

: Argument to append to Job args list.

name

: Option to specify a name related to this argument. If a name is
  specified, then a separate set of log, output, and error files
  will be generated for this particular argument
  (default is `None`).

  :::{versionadded} 0.1.2
  :::

retry

: Option to specify the number of times to retry this node. Default
  number of retries is 0. Note: this feature is only available to
  Jobs that are submitted via a Dagman.

  :::{versionadded} 0.1.2
  :::

# Returns

self

: Returns self.

documentation:
Adds multiple arguments to Job

Parameters
----------
args : iterable
    Iterable of arguments to append to the arguments list

Returns
-------
self : object
    Returns self.

methods.name: pycondor.job.Job.build
documentation:
Build and saves the submit file for Job

# Parameters

makedirs

: If Job directories (e.g. error, output, log, submit) don't exist,
  create them (default is `True`).

fancyname

: Appends the date and unique id number to error, log, output, and
  submit files. For example, instead of `jobname.submit` the submit
  file becomes `jobname_YYYYMMD_id`. This is useful when running
  several Jobs of the same name (default is `True`).

# Returns

self

: Returns self.

methods.name: pycondor.job.Job.submit_job
documentation:
Submits Job to condor

# Parameters

submit_options

: Options to be passed to `condor_submit` for this Job
  (see the [condor_submit documentation](http://research.cs.wisc.edu/htcondor/manual/current/condor_submit.html)
  for possible options).

# Returns

self

: Returns self.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import pycondor
>>> job = pycondor.Job('myjob', 'myscript.py')
>>> job.build()
>>> job.submit_job(submit_options='-maxjobs 1000 -interactive')
```

methods.name: pycondor.job.Job.build_submit
documentation:
Calls build and submit sequentially

# Parameters

makedirs

: If Job directories (e.g. error, output, log, submit) don't exist,
  create them (default is `True`).

fancyname

: Appends the date and unique id number to error, log, output, and
  submit files. For example, instead of `jobname.submit` the submit
  file becomes `jobname_YYYYMMD_id`. This is useful when running
  several Jobs of the same name (default is `True`).

submit_options

: Options to be passed to `condor_submit` for this Job
  (see the [condor_submit documentation](http://research.cs.wisc.edu/htcondor/manual/current/condor_submit.html)
  for possible options).

# Returns

self

: Returns self.

Job object consisting of an executable to be run, potentially with a
series of different command-line arguments.

Note that the `submit`, `error`, `log`, and `output` parameters
can be explicitly given or configured by setting `PYCONDOR_SUBMIT_DIR`,
`PYCONDOR_ERROR_DIR`, `PYCONDOR_LOG_DIR`, and `PYCONDOR_OUTPUT_DIR`
environment variables. An explicitly given value will be used over an
environment variable, while an environment variable will be used over a
default value.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the Job instance. This will also be the name of the
  corresponding error, log, output, and submit files associated with
  this Job.

executable

: Path to corresponding executable for Job.

error

: Path to directory where condor Job error files will be written (default
  is None, will not be included in Job submit file).

log

: Path to directory where condor Job log files will be written (default
  is None, will not be included in Job submit file).

output

: Path to directory where condor Job output files will be written
  (default is None, will not be included in Job submit file).

submit

: Path to directory where condor Job submit files will be written
  (defaults to the directory was the Job was submitted from).

request_memory

: Memory request to be included in submit file.

request_disk

: Disk request to be included in submit file.

request_cpus

: Number of CPUs to request in submit file.

  :::{versionadded} 0.1.0
  :::

getenv

: Whether or not to use the current environment settings when running
  the job (default is None).

universe

: Universe execution environment to be specified in submit file
  (default is None).

initialdir

: Initial directory for relative paths (defaults to the directory was
  the job was submitted from).

notification

: E-mail notification preference (default is None).

requirements

: Additional requirements to be included in ClassAd.

queue

: Integer specifying how many times you would like this job to run.

extra_lines

: List of additional lines to be added to submit file.

dag

: If specified, Job will be added to dag (default is None).

arguments

: Arguments with which to initialize the Job list of arguments
  (default is None).

retry

: Option to specify the number of retries for all Job arguments. This
  can be superseded for arguments added via the add_arg() method.
  Note: this feature is only available to Jobs that are submitted via a
  Dagman (default is None; no retries).

verbose

: Level of logging verbosity option are 0-warning, 1-info,
  2-debugging (default is 0).

# Attributes

args

: List of arguments for this Job instance.

parents

: Only set when included in a Dagman. List of parent Jobs and Dagmans.
  Ensures that Jobs and Dagmans in the parents list will complete
  before this Job is submitted to HTCondor.

children

: Only set when included in a Dagman. List of child Jobs and Dagmans.
  Ensures that Jobs and Dagmans in the children list will be
  submitted only after this Job has completed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import pycondor
>>> job = pycondor.Job('myjob', 'myscript.py')
>>> job.build_submit()
```
